workflow.name: Recover abandoned WooCommerce carts with custom coupon emails
workflow.description:
**Stop paying for expensive plugins to recover your valuable revenue from abandoned carts on your WooCommerce store**

## How It Works?
1. When a product is added to a user's cart on your store, it fetches the cart contents via webhook & it utilises the code provided in the red sticky note to fetch the required info.
2. It waits for a specified time to allow the user to place an order.
3. It checks if the order has been placed or not.
4. It creates the HTML with dynamic information fetched from previous nodes.
5. It sends the email to the user via configured SMTP credentials.

## Setup Steps (20 minutes):
1. Set up your WooCommerce Account Credentials in n8n
2. Set up webhook in n8n & WooCommerce
3. Add the provided code in functions.php or as a PHP snippet via a plugin onto your website
4. Customize the coupon code's phrase according to your needs
5.  Customize the email's HTML code according to your needs


## Requirements

- **WooCommerce Store**: With REST API access enabled.

- **SMTP Credentials**: For sending recovery emails.

For any queries, you can ping me on [X](https://x.com/matta_kshitij)

workflow.name: Recover abandoned carts with custom coupons for WooCommerce

nodes.name: Fetching If Customer has placed order

parameters.subject: =Greetings! You left [Your Product Name] in your cart!

parameters.content:
# Recover abandoned carts with custom coupons for WooCommerce


This n8n workflow helps you recover lost sales opportunities by automatically sending personalized emails with a customized coupon to your customers who leave the website after adding products to their carts without completing checkout.


## How it works :

-  Webhook Trigger:  Webhook receives complete cart details from WooCommerce the moment any customer adds an item to the cart.

- Verification of Order: It waits for the specified wait time and then checks through the WooCommerce API node to see if the customer has actually completed the purchase during that window.

- Coupon Generation: If no order is found, a Code node generates a unique, custom coupon code with a custom expiry time (e.g., 30 minutes).

-  Coupon Creation on Website: An HTTP Request node registers this new coupon directly into your WooCommerce store via the REST API.

-  Email Composition: A Code node fetches all information extracted and converts it into an HTML email template.

-  Sending Emails: Finally, the SMTP node sends the personalized recovery mail to the customer's email address.

## Setup Steps :

1. Set up your WooCommerce Account Credentials in n8n
2. Set up Webhook in n8n & WooCommerce
3. Add the provided code in functions.php or as a PHP snippet via a plugin in your website
4. Customize the coupon code's phrase according to your needs
5.  Customize the email's HTML code according to your needs


## Requirements :

- WooCommerce Store: With WooCommerce REST API access enabled.

- SMTP Credentials: For sending recovery emails.


## Need Help?

[Schedule a 1:1 Troubleshooting Call](https://cal.com/mattakshitij/workflow-troubleshoot) or [Ping me on X](https://x.com/matta_kshitij)

Happy Automating :)

## 1. Configuring webhook in n8n and WooCommerce

Set up the webhook path and configure it in WooCommerce. Follow the given steps:
- Go to WooCommerce Settings > Advanced > Webhook
- Create a webhook and select Topic as Action and Action Event as woocommerce_add_to_cart
- Add Webhook URL configured in n8n in the Delivery URL field

##  2. Add the provided code to your WP (WooCommerce) website to enable the features of fetching cart details and coupon code customization

** This PHP Code Snippet needs to be added in either the functions.php code of your theme or can be added via code snippet plugins like HFCM, which enables code to run on all pages of the website.

- Access the code from [here](https://pastebin.com/tp0yFSRN)
- Add it to your WordPress (WooCommerce) website

## 3. Customize the coupon code according to your needs

- In COUPON_PREFIX, you need to set the phrase of the coupon, for example, your coupon to be HELLOxxx, then you need to put it in HELLO.
- In EXPIRY_MINUTES, set the time in minutes for which the coupon should be active.
- In TIMEZONE, you need to set the timezone acc. to your WP website's Timezone

## 4. Customize email HTML according to your needs


- Add HTML Code and set the variables defined in it to fetch values dynamically.

- In TIMEZONE, you need to set the timezone according to your WP website's Timezone

- CURRENCY_SYMBOL Add the symbol of the currency used in your store

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
